Vocabulary
🔍
Absolutely verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
Completely; without doubt
"I absolutely agree with your opinion in this debate."
Academic adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Relating to education or study
"She received an academic award for her research."
Access noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The ability or right to use or enter something
"Students have access to online resources for homework."
Accommodation noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A place to live or stay
"We found cheap accommodation near the station."
Account noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A record of money or a written report
"Please check your bank account carefully."
Achievement noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Something you succeed in doing
"Winning the competition was a great achievement."
Act verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To behave in a certain way
"We must act quickly to solve this problem."
Addition noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The act of adding something
"The addition of new rules improved safety."
Admire verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To respect or think highly of someone
"I admire people who speak confidently in public."
Admit verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To accept that something is true
"He admitted that his argument was weak."
Advanced adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
At a high or difficult level
"She is taking an advanced English class."
Advise verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To give suggestions or guidance
"Teachers advise students about their future plans."
Afford verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To have enough money or time for something
"Many students cannot afford expensive textbooks."
Age noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The number of years someone has lived
"At the age of sixteen, she started working part-time."
Agent noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A person who represents or works for others
"The travel agent booked our tickets."
Agreement noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A decision accepted by all sides
"The two teams reached an agreement after discussion."
Ahead verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
In front; in the future
"The finish line is just ahead."
Aim noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A goal or purpose
"Her aim is to improve her speaking skills."
Alarm noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A warning sound or signal
"The fire alarm rang loudly."
Album noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A collection of photos or music
"She showed me her wedding album."
Alcohol noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A drink containing ethanol
"The law forbids selling alcohol to minors."
Alternative noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Another choice or option
"We need an alternative to plastic bags."
Amazed adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Very surprised
"I was amazed by her confident speech."
Ambition noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A strong desire to achieve something
"His ambition is to become a lawyer."
Ambitious adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Having a strong desire for success
"She is ambitious and studies every night."
Analyse verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To examine something carefully
"We must analyse both sides of the issue."
Analysis noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A detailed examination
"Her analysis of the data was clear and logical."
Announce verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To make something known publicly
"The teacher will announce the winner tomorrow."
Announcement noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A public statement
"The announcement surprised everyone."
Annoy verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To make someone slightly angry
"Loud talking can annoy other students."
Annoyed adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Feeling slightly angry
"She felt annoyed by the unfair comment."
Annoying adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Causing slight anger
"The annoying noise distracted the class."
Apart verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
Separated by distance or time
"The two houses are far apart."
Apologise verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To say sorry
"I apologise for interrupting your speech."
Application noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A formal request
"She submitted her university application."
Appointment noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
An arranged meeting
"I have a dentist appointment today."
Appreciate verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To feel grateful for something
"I appreciate your honest feedback."
Approximately verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
About; nearly
"The trip takes approximately three hours."
Arrest verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To take someone to the police
"Police arrested the suspect last night."
Arrival noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The act of coming to a place
"Her arrival was later than expected."
Assignment noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A task given to someone
"The assignment is due on Monday."
Assist verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To help someone
"The teacher will assist you if you have questions."
Atmosphere noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The mood of a place
"The debate had a serious atmosphere."
Attach verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To connect or fasten
"Please attach your file to the email."
Attitude noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A way of thinking or feeling
"A positive attitude helps in debates."
Attract verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To draw attention
"Good examples attract the audience’s interest."
Attraction noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Something that draws interest
"The castle is a popular tourist attraction."
Authority noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The power to give orders
"Teachers have authority in the classroom."
Average adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Usual or typical
"The average score was 75 percent."
Award noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A prize for achievement
"She won an award for her speech."
Aware adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Knowing about something
"Students should be aware of the rules."
Backwards verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
Toward the back
"He stepped backwards carefully."
Bake verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To cook in an oven
"She baked a cake for the event."
Balance noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A state of equal importance
"It is important to keep a balance between study and rest."
Ban verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To officially forbid
"The school will ban phones during exams."
Bank noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The side of a river
"We had a picnic on the river bank."
Base noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The bottom part of something
"The statue stands on a stone base."
Basic adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Simple and fundamental
"Basic grammar is necessary for debate."
Basis noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The main reason or foundation
"Trust is the basis of teamwork."
Battery noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A device that stores electricity
"My phone battery is almost dead."
Battle noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A fight between groups
"The battle lasted for hours."
Beauty noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The quality of being beautiful
"The beauty of the scene impressed everyone."
Bee noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A flying insect that makes honey
"A bee landed on the flower."
Belief noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
An idea you think is true
"His belief in fairness shaped his argument."
Bell noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A device that rings
"The school bell rang loudly."
Bend verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To curve something
"Do not bend the paper."
Benefit noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
An advantage or good result
"There are many benefits to regular exercise."
Better adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Of higher quality
"This solution is better than the first one."
Bite verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To cut with your teeth
"The dog tried to bite the toy."
Block noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A large building
"She lives in the next apartment block."
Board noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A flat surface for writing
"Write your answer on the board."
Bomb noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
An explosive weapon
"The police found a fake bomb."
Border noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The line between two countries
"They crossed the border safely."
Bother verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To cause trouble or worry
"Don’t bother me while I’m studying."
Branch noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A part of a tree
"A bird sat on the branch."
Brand noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A type of product made by a company
"This brand is popular among students."
Brave adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Showing courage
"The brave girl spoke in front of everyone."
Breath noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The air you take into your lungs
"Take a deep breath before speaking."
Breathe verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To take air into your lungs
"It is hard to breathe at high altitudes."
Breathing noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The act of taking in air
"Deep breathing helps you relax."
Bride noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A woman on her wedding day
"The bride looked very happy."
Bubble noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A ball of air in liquid
"The child blew a big bubble."
Bury verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To put something under the ground
"They buried the treasure underground."
Calm adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Peaceful and quiet
"She remained calm during the debate."

An organized effort to achieve something
"The campaign aimed to reduce waste."
Campus noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The grounds of a university or school
"The campus is full of trees."
Candidate noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A person applying for a position
"Each candidate gave a short speech."
Cap noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A soft hat
"He wore a cap in the sun."
Captain noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The leader of a team
"The captain encouraged the team."
Careless adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Not careful
"Careless mistakes lower your score."
Category noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A group with similar features
"The books are sorted by category."
Ceiling noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The top inside surface of a room
"The ceiling is painted white."
Celebration noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A special event to mark something
"They held a celebration after winning."
Central adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
In the middle; most important
"The central issue is education."

The middle point
"We met at the city centre."
Ceremony noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A formal event
"The graduation ceremony was emotional."
Chain noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A series of connected links
"The bike chain needs fixing."
Challenge noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A difficult task
"This topic is a real challenge."
Champion noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The winner of a competition
"She became the debate champion."
Channel noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A television station
"I changed the channel to watch the news."
Comparison noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The act of examining how things are similar or different
"The comparison between the two plans showed clear differences."
Competitor noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A person or company that competes with others
"Our competitor lowered their prices last month."
Competitive adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Wanting to win; involving competition
"The university entrance exam is highly competitive."
Complaint noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A statement that something is wrong or unfair
"She made a complaint about the poor service."
Complex adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Having many parts; difficult to understand
"The issue is too complex to explain quickly."
Concentrate verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To focus your attention
"Please concentrate on your writing task."
Conclude verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To end something; to decide after thinking
"We concluded that the plan was effective."
Conclusion noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The final part or decision
"In conclusion, we support this proposal."
Confident adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Feeling sure about your ability
"She felt confident before her speech."
Confirm verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To state that something is true or correct
"Please confirm your email address."
Confuse verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To make someone unable to understand
"Difficult words can confuse readers."
Confused adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Not understanding clearly
"I am confused about the instructions."
Connection noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A relationship or link between things
"There is a strong connection between diet and health."
Consequence noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A result of an action
"Poor preparation has serious consequences."
Consist verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To be formed from something
"The committee consists of ten members."
Consume verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To use up something
"This machine consumes a lot of electricity."
Consumer noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A person who buys goods or services
"Consumers want safe and affordable products."
Contact verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To communicate with someone
"Please contact the office for more information."
Contain verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To have something inside
"This book contains useful advice."
Content adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Feeling satisfied and happy
"She felt content with her results."
Contest noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A competition
"He entered a speech contest at school."
Continue verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To keep doing something
"The teacher asked her to continue speaking."
Contract noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A legal written agreement
"They signed a contract with the company."
Control verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To manage or direct something
"It is hard to control a large group."
Conversation noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
An informal talk between people
"We had a long conversation about the topic."
Cook verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To prepare food with heat
"She learned to cook traditional meals."
Cool adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Slightly cold; calm
"The evening air felt cool and fresh."

A duplicate of something
"Please make a copy of this report."
Corner noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The place where two lines or streets meet
"The shop is on the corner of the street."

Right; without mistakes
"Please give the correct answer."

The amount of money needed
"The cost of food is increasing."

To say numbers in order
"Can you count from one to twenty?"
Country noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A nation with its own government
"Japan is a safe country."

Two people together
"The couple celebrated their anniversary."
Course noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A series of lessons on a subject
"She is taking an English course."
Cover verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To put something over another thing
"Please cover the table with a cloth."
Create verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To make something new
"Students create presentations for class."
Creative adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Using imagination to make new ideas
"Creative solutions are often the best."

An action that breaks the law
"The city is working to reduce crime."

A time of serious difficulty
"The country faced an economic crisis."

Comments about mistakes or problems
"She accepted criticism and improved her work."

To go from one side to another
"Look both ways before you cross the road."

A large group of people
"A crowd gathered in the square."

The traditions and way of life of a group
"Food is an important part of culture."

A small container for drinking
"She drank a cup of tea."
Curious adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Wanting to know more
"The students were curious about the experiment."
Current adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Happening now
"The current situation is serious."
Customer noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A person who buys something
"The store values each customer."

To divide with a knife or sharp tool
"He cut the paper carefully."

Harm or injury
"The storm caused serious damage."

To move to music
"They danced at the party."

The possibility of harm
"There is danger in driving too fast."

Likely to cause harm
"It is dangerous to swim here."

Without light
"The sky became dark quickly."

Facts or information used for study
"The data supports our argument."
Date noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A particular day of the month or year
"What is today’s date?"
Deal noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
An agreement between people
"They made a deal after negotiation."
Death noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The end of life
"The news of his death shocked everyone."
Debate noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A formal discussion with different opinions

Money that you owe

To choose something
"We must decide quickly."

A choice that has been made
"It was a difficult decision."
Declare verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To announce officially
"The leader declared the results."
Decorate verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To make something look attractive
"They decorated the hall for the event."
Deep adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Going far down

To win against someone
"Our team defeated the champions."

A university qualification
"She earned a degree in law."

A situation in which something happens late

To bring something to someone

To ask for something strongly
"Workers demand fair pay."

A section of a company or organization

To rely on someone or something
"Children depend on adults."

To explain what something is like

Words that explain something

A person who creates designs

A strong wish
"She has a strong desire to succeed."

A table for writing or working
"My books are on the desk."

Without being affected by
"Despite the rain, the game continued."

To damage completely
"The fire destroyed the house."

A small piece of information
"Check every detail carefully."

To grow or improve
"She developed her speaking skills."

Growth or progress
"Economic development is important."

A machine or tool for a purpose
"This device saves energy."

The food someone usually eats
"A healthy diet is important."

The way things are not the same
"There is a big difference between them."

Not the same
"We have different opinions."

Hard to do
"This question is difficult."

A problem; something hard to do

Going straight; clear

The way to go

A person who manages an organization

Not clean
"His shoes were dirty."

To go out of sight
"The sun disappeared behind clouds."

To find something new

The act of finding something new

To talk about something

A conversation about a topic

An illness
"Smoking can cause disease."
Display verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To show something publicly
"The museum displays rare paintings."

The space between two points or places

To give something out to many people
"Volunteers distribute food to people in need."

An area of a city or country
"This district is famous for its restaurants."

To separate into parts or groups
"The teacher divided the class into teams."

An official written paper

Related to a country’s own area; within one country
"Domestic travel is cheaper than international travel."

To become twice as much
"The company hopes to double its sales this year."

A feeling of uncertainty
"I have no doubt about her ability."

To move data from the internet to your device
"Students can download the worksheet online."

A serious play or emotional event
"She joined the school drama club."

To make a picture with a pen or pencil
"He likes to draw cartoons."
Dream noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A strong wish or hope for the future
"Her dream is to study abroad."

To control and operate a car
"She learned to drive last year."

To let something fall
"Be careful not to drop your phone."

A medicine or illegal substance
"The doctor gave her a new drug."

Expected to happen at a certain time
"The report is due tomorrow."

Throughout the time of something
"Please stay quiet during the exam."
Duty noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Something you must do
"It is your duty to follow the rules."
Each noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Every one separately
"Each student received a certificate."
Earn verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To receive money for work
"She earns money by teaching online."
Earth noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The planet we live on
"Earth orbits the sun."

"The country faces economic problems."

The system of trade and business in a country
"Tourism supports the local economy."

The outside limit or border
"He stood on the edge of the cliff."
Edit verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To correct and improve writing
"Please edit your essay carefully."
Education noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The process of learning and teaching
"Education helps people achieve their goals."
Effect noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A result or change caused by something
"The new rule had a positive effect."

Producing the intended result
"This method is very effective."
Effort noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Hard work or attempt
"She made a great effort to improve."

A process of choosing leaders by voting
"The election will take place next month."

Using electricity or digital technology
"Electronic devices are common today."

A basic part of something
"Trust is an important element of teamwork."

A sudden dangerous situation
"Call for help in an emergency."
Emotion noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A strong feeling
"Anger is a natural emotion."

Showing strong feelings

A person who works for a company

A person or company that gives jobs
"The employer offered good benefits."
Employment noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Having paid work
"Employment levels are rising."
Empty adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Having nothing inside
"The box was completely empty."
Enable verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To make something possible
"The scholarship enabled her to study abroad."

To support or give confidence
"Teachers encourage students to speak up."
End noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The final part of something
"The movie had a surprising end."

A person who is against you
"The soldiers fought the enemy."
Energy noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Power to do work or move
"Solar panels produce clean energy."
Engine noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A machine that produces power
"The engine stopped suddenly."
Engineer noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A person who designs or builds machines
"The engineer designed a safe bridge."
Enjoy verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To like something and feel happy about it
"I enjoy discussing ideas in class."
Enormous adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Very large in size or amount
"The stadium is enormous."
Enough noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
As much as necessary
"We have enough time to finish."
Enter verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To go into a place
"Please enter the room quietly."
Entertainment noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Activities that make people enjoy themselves
"The festival offers live entertainment."
Entire adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Whole; complete
"She read the entire article."
Environment noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The natural world around us
"We must protect the environment."

Related to nature and the environment
"Environmental problems are serious."
Equal adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
The same in value or amount
"All students should have equal opportunities."
Equipment noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Tools or machines used for a purpose
"The lab has modern equipment."
Error noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
A mistake
"There is an error in your answer."
Escape verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To get away from danger
"The prisoners tried to escape."
Especially verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
More than others
"I like fruit, especially apples."
Essential adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Very necessary or important
"Water is essential for life."
Establish verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To start or set up
"They established a new company."
Estate noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Land or property
"He works in real estate."
Estimate verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
To guess roughly the amount
"Can you estimate the cost?"
European adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
From Europe
"She studies European history."
Even verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
Used to emphasize something surprising
"Even beginners can understand this."
Evening noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
The time between afternoon and night
"We met in the evening."
Event noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Something that happens
"The sports event was successful."
Eventually verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
In the end; after some time
"She eventually solved the problem."
Ever verb Eiken 2 - B1 500
At any time
"Have you ever tried sushi?"
Every noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
All without exception
"Every student passed the test."
Evidence noun Eiken 2 - B1 500
Facts that show something is true
"There is strong evidence to support the claim."
Exact adjective Eiken 2 - B1 500
Completely correct
"Please tell me the exact number."
